opening_excerpt:
The ranking went out and you're still near the top. Your jaw clenched at the phrase still near the top.

You got the promotion and your internal valuation went up. The deal fell through and it went down. You are running a constant market valuation on yourself. Your worth fluctuates with the external signals. When things are winning, you feel valuable. When things stall, you feel diminished. Your sense of yourself has become a traded security. This is the exit multiple on yourself—the internal valuation that adjusts with market conditions, the sense of worth that is tethered to outcomes instead of being stable.

That's the exit multiple on yourself. It will come up throughout this book — not because it's the only thing happening, but because it tends to be underneath most of what we're going to look at.

You shift your measurement. Instead of rank, you measure differently. You measure by impact. You measure by the people you've helped. You measure by the work that matters. {Queen Street West is where the impact is visible. lake mist dulling the skyline is the change in perspective. {Line 1 southbound brings people who've been helped. The people are more than the rank. The impact is more than the ranking. The ranking is one measurement. The impact is another measurement. {Queen Street West measures better. Impact measures better. You're measuring better. Better measurement makes fourth less important.

That pause is doing more than slowing you down. It is showing you how the pattern enters, which means the chapter can name it before it hardens.

The watcher is the shape anxiety takes when it turns inward. You watch yourself performing, then watch yourself watching, then evaluate how well you stopped watching. The recursion is infinite. Each layer creates a new layer to observe.

The point is that the exit multiple on yourself treats every decision like a permanent threat.

I have come to understand that there is a particular version of self that emerges when you are not being observed. When there is no audience. When the performance can stop. This self is different from the self that exists in the role. The self in the role is constructed. It is built from the title, the achievements, the recognition. It is the self that the external world sees.
